drink_man.name: Abita Amber
drink_man.description: Cold Louisiana craft lager. The Cat is cash-only, no-frills — locals drink cheap cold beer and sway to the jazz.

drink_woman.name: Rum & Coke
drink_woman.description: Simple, sweet, and easy. This is a listen-to-the-music spot, not a cocktail lounge.

custom_drink.name: The Faubourg Swing
custom_drink.description: Rye whiskey, Creole shrub (apple cider vinegar, cane sugar, local fruit), dash of bitters, topped with soda water — named for the Faubourg Marigny.

drink_man.name: Draft Beer (Abita or Dixie)
drink_man.description: One of Bourbon Street's oldest jazz clubs, German-owned. A cold draft is the move.

drink_woman.name: Kir Royale
drink_woman.description: Champagne with a splash of crème de cassis — fits the European pub heritage with a touch of elegance.

custom_drink.name: The Storyville Schnapps
custom_drink.description: German schnapps base, muddled Louisiana strawberry, squeeze of lemon, float of local honey — a nod to Fritzel's German roots and New Orleans soul.

drink_man.name: Dark & Stormy
drink_man.description: Dark rum and ginger beer over ice. Bamboula's has a Caribbean soul — this drink matches the vibe.

drink_woman.name: Mango Rum Punch
drink_woman.description: Tropical rum punch with mango, pineapple, and grenadine — Caribbean-inspired at this Frenchmen St staple.

custom_drink.name: The Congo Square
custom_drink.description: Haitian rum, tamarind syrup, fresh lime, coconut water, dash of Angostura — a tribute to New Orleans' African and Caribbean heritage.

drink_man.name: Hurricane
drink_man.description: The original and iconic NOLA cocktail — Pat O's own recipe with light & dark rum, passion fruit syrup, lemon juice, in the signature hurricane glass. Born here in the 1940s.

drink_woman.name: Cyclone
drink_woman.description: A lighter, sweeter cousin to the Hurricane — different rum ratios and citrus, offered as an alternative on the menu.

custom_drink.name: The Bourbon Street Blow
custom_drink.description: Light rum, overproof rum float, hibiscus syrup, pineapple juice, splash of grenadine — served in a souvenir glass with a flamed orange peel.

drink_man.name: Cold Beer
drink_man.description: Preservation Hall is about the music above all else. The bar is minimal — cold canned beer is typically what's available. Drink it standing in reverence.

drink_woman.name: House Wine
drink_woman.description: Simple red or white house wine. 726 St. Peter — all that matters is the music.

custom_drink.name: The Second Line
custom_drink.description: Bourbon, sweet tea, muddled mint, splash of peach nectar — a sippin' tribute to the jazz ancestors who built New Orleans culture.

drink_man.name: Old Fashioned
drink_man.description: Snug Harbor is a full craft bar and bistro. A proper bourbon Old Fashioned matches the sophisticated jazz atmosphere.

drink_woman.name: Champagne Cocktail
drink_woman.description: Bubbly with a sugar cube soaked in Angostura bitters — elegant and classic for Snug's upscale jazz bistro setting.

custom_drink.name: The Marigny Mist
custom_drink.description: St-Germain elderflower, sparkling wine, cucumber ribbon, fresh mint, splash of grapefruit — light and jazz-club chic.

drink_man.name: Côtes du Rhône (Natural Red)
drink_man.description: Bacchanal is a natural wine bar first. A robust southern Rhône red — grenache, syrah, mourvèdre — pairs perfectly with backyard music and cheese plates.

drink_woman.name: Pét-Nat Rosé
drink_woman.description: Slightly fizzy, funky, and beautiful. Bacchanal's rotating list always has a killer pét-nat for warm New Orleans evenings.

custom_drink.name: The Bywater Botanist
custom_drink.description: Elderflower gin, dry vermouth, muddled basil, squeeze of lemon, topped with natural sparkling wine — garden-fresh for Bacchanal's backyard vibe.

drink_man.name: Whiskey Neat
drink_man.description: d.b.a. is legendary for its whiskey selection — one of the best in New Orleans. A serious bourbon or rye poured neat is the definitive d.b.a. experience.

drink_woman.name: Craft Beer (Rotating Draft)
drink_woman.description: d.b.a. also boasts an exceptional craft beer list — they rotate Louisiana and regional craft options constantly.

custom_drink.name: The Frenchmen Street
custom_drink.description: Rye whiskey, Averna amaro, dash of orange bitters, expressed orange peel — stirred and spirit-forward for a bar that takes its booze seriously.

drink_man.name: Bourbon on the Rocks
drink_man.description: It's literally in the name. Maison Bourbon on Bourbon Street — straight bourbon over ice at one of the oldest jazz clubs on the strip.

drink_woman.name: Bourbon Sour
drink_woman.description: Bourbon, fresh lemon, simple syrup, egg white foam — classic and smooth for an evening of nonstop jazz.

custom_drink.name: The Connick Jr.
custom_drink.description: Sazerac-style: rye, Peychaud's bitters, absinthe rinse, lemon peel — a nod to Harry Connick Jr. who apprenticed here. Pure New Orleans tradition.

drink_man.name: Abita Purple Haze
drink_man.description: Louisiana's classic raspberry wheat beer, cold and on draft. Rock 'n' Bowl is a fun, unpretentious bowling alley/music venue — this purple beer fits perfectly.

drink_woman.name: Frozen Daiquiri
drink_woman.description: New Orleans frozen daiquiris are a way of life. Rock 'n' Bowl keeps frozen machines running — strawberry or mango are crowd favorites.

custom_drink.name: The Zydeco Strike
custom_drink.description: Bourbon, muddled watermelon, fresh lime, jalapeño slice, agave syrup — bold, fun, and a little spicy, like a zydeco set at midnight.

drink_man.name: Budweiser Tall Boy
drink_man.description: BJ's is a Bywater institution and true New Orleans dive bar. Ice cold cheap beer in a can. No pretension allowed.

drink_woman.name: Purple Drank
drink_woman.description: BJ's legendary house special — grain alcohol, grape drink, served in a large cup. Notoriously potent. A Bywater rite of passage.

custom_drink.name: The Bywater Burglar
custom_drink.description: Everclear, watermelon Kool-Aid, squeeze of lime, crushed ice — dangerously easy to drink, served in a 32oz Styrofoam cup.

drink_man.name: Sazerac
drink_man.description: New Orleans' official cocktail — rye whiskey, Peychaud's bitters, absinthe rinse, sugar cube. A proper Sazerac at a Frenchmen St staple.

drink_woman.name: Passion Fruit Rum Punch
drink_woman.description: Blue Nile has a full bar and eclectic vibe. A tropical rum punch with passion fruit and citrus fits the energy of the upstairs stage.

custom_drink.name: The Blue Nile Ritual
custom_drink.description: Hendrick's gin, muddled blueberry, lavender syrup, lemon juice, splash of tonic — purple-hued and funky, like the music downstairs.

drink_man.name: Hurricane
drink_man.description: You're on a steamboat on the Mississippi with a jazz band playing — of course you order a Hurricane. Rum, passion fruit, citrus, grenadine in the iconic glass.

drink_woman.name: Mint Julep
drink_woman.description: Bourbon, fresh mint, simple syrup, crushed ice — a Southern classic perfectly suited to a steamboat cruise at sunset.

custom_drink.name: The River Queen
custom_drink.description: Aged rum, peach schnapps, fresh peach purée, lemon juice, topped with sparkling wine — luxurious and riverboat-worthy.

drink_man.name: Jack & Coke
drink_man.description: Chickie Wah Wah is a Mid-City rock/R&B/soul venue. An honest bar — Jack Daniel's and Coke is the right call while catching a band.

drink_woman.name: Tito's Vodka Soda
drink_woman.description: Clean, simple, sessionable for a full evening of live music. Chickie Wah Wah draws a local crowd that keeps it real.

custom_drink.name: The Wah Wah Pedal
custom_drink.description: Bourbon, house-made sweet tea, fresh lemon, raspberry syrup, splash of ginger beer — Southern and electric, like a slide guitar on the upswing.

drink_man.name: Craft IPA
drink_man.description: Gasa Gasa is an intimate Uptown indie/alternative venue. A hoppy local craft IPA is the right vibe for the artsy crowd.

drink_woman.name: Natural Wine
drink_woman.description: Orange wine or skin-contact white — very Gasa Gasa, very now. They rotate interesting natural pours.

custom_drink.name: The Uptown Haze
custom_drink.description: Mezcal, grapefruit, honey, rosemary smoke — artsy and a little weird, in the best way.

drink_man.name: Tequila Neat
drink_man.description: Dos Jefes is an Uptown cigar bar and jazz club. Blanco tequila with a lime wheel — straight and simple at this legendary spot.

drink_woman.name: Margarita
drink_woman.description: Fresh lime, good tequila, salt rim — classic and done right in one of New Orleans' best late-night cigar bars.

custom_drink.name: El Jefe Smash
custom_drink.description: Reposado tequila, muddled jalapeño, pineapple, lime — spicy like the late-night sets and the cigar smoke curling toward the ceiling.

drink_man.name: Abita Amber on Tap
drink_man.description: Buffa's is a Marigny neighborhood institution open 24/7 — local beer, always cold, always on draft.

drink_woman.name: Bloody Mary
drink_woman.description: Their Bloody Mary is legendary — spicy, thick, and perfect before noon or after midnight. Buffa's keeps no particular hours.

custom_drink.name: The Esplanade Slide
custom_drink.description: Bourbon, sweet tea, lemon, mint — lazy afternoon on the Esplanade median vibes, stretched into evening.

drink_man.name: Jägermeister Shot
drink_man.description: Vaughan's is Bywater's legendary dive — where the Thursday night trumpet shakes the walls and nobody's putting on airs.

drink_woman.name: Whatever's Cold in the Cooler
drink_woman.description: Vaughan's is about the music, not the menu. Grab whatever's in the ice chest and get your ass on the dance floor.

custom_drink.name: The Kermit Punch
custom_drink.description: Dark rum, ginger beer, blackberry, lime — as funky as Kermit Ruffins himself, the Thursday night legend at Vaughan's for years.

drink_man.name: Jäger Bomb
drink_man.description: Le Bon Temps is an Uptown dive bar classic on Magazine Street — they don't pretend otherwise and neither should you.

drink_woman.name: Frozen Daiquiri
drink_woman.description: To-go cup, frozen daiquiri, Magazine Street — quintessentially New Orleans. Let the good times roll.

custom_drink.name: Laissez Les Bons Temps
custom_drink.description: Rum, mango, passion fruit, soda, grenadine sunset — because that's the whole point.
